result:
Pre-registered iteration 14 (experiment 35) in `runs/pending_experiment.json`.

**Pivot rationale:** the last 9 experiments (26–34) covered capacity scaling, readout resolution, lighting dispatch, coordinate reparameterization, risk calibration, augmentation, and margin loss — all reverted (two of them, 33/34, never actually trained due to disk/OOM crashes per prior session's forensics). Per the harness's patience note, this iteration opens a genuinely untried family: **training-data sampling strategy**, not touching `model/model.py` at all.

**The change:** a one-time, per-area, offline computation (in `train.py` only) that pools `reference.tif` into the same 32×32 grid the classifier scores, finds each cell's nearest *distant* look-alike by simple RGB/luminance descriptor distance, and turns that into a sampling weight — so cells with a confusable twin elsewhere on the map get drawn roughly 2× as often during training (blended 50/50 with the existing uniform draw, so full-map coverage is preserved). This directly targets the champion's diagnosed bottleneck (true cell ranks top-1 on only 2% of eval crops, usually beaten by a lookalike elsewhere) without repeating any of the last 9 mechanisms.

Calibration sampling is explicitly left uniform/untouched to avoid confounding the result with the known coverage–median coupling issue. `autoresearch.figcheck` passes on the architecture figure. The harness will now implement, train, and score this from `runs/pending_experiment.json`.

tool_input.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
svg = sqlite3.connect('experiments.sqlite').execute(\"SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=33\").fetchone()[0]
open('/tmp/exp33.svg','w').write(svg)
print(len(svg))
"
